articles.title: Forget Bengal, we will dethrone BJP from Delhi: CM Mamata Banerjee after phase-1 turnout
articles.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee declared the high phase-one voter turnout a decisive mandate against the BJP, predicting the saffron party would lose significantly. She accused the BJP of atrocities and warned of "democratic revenge" for their actions, urging focus on Manipur's unrest and criticizing their treatment of allies like Nitish Kumar.

articles.title: Delhi Confidential: Meal, Mellow
articles.description: Senior Advocate Kalyan Bandopadhyay, training guns on the ECI represented by Senior Advocate D S Naidu who too hit back, it was left to CJI Surya Kant, Justice Joymalya Bagchi and Solicitor General Tushar Mehta to cool tempers.

articles.title: Saying f*** off at work ‘uncouth’, not sexual harassment, rules Punjab and Haryana High Court
UAE, Russia FMs Discuss Bilateral Ties, Regional De-escalation
articles.description: UAE's Foreign Minister, Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan, and his Russian counterpart, Sergey Lavrov, held a phone call. They discussed bilateral relations, mutual interests, regional challenges, and ways to promote de-escalation and peace.

articles.title: India will become a world leader in this lifetime: RSS chief Bhagwat
articles.description: RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat says holy saints have predicted India will become a world leader (Vishwaguru). He urged citizens to perform Bharatdurga Mandir puja daily and take small steps to reclaim India's identity and witness its greatness.

articles.title: HC strikes down EC curbs on using motorcycles in Bengal
articles.description: The Calcutta High Court ruled that the Election Commission cannot impose a blanket ban on motorcycle commuting in West Bengal during elections.

articles.title: I couldn’t have children – but being an aunt has been its own kind of joy
articles.description: Balvinder Sandhu never had children of her own but embraced life as a PANK – Professional Aunt, No Kids – devoting time, love and yes, money to her niece and nephew. She reflects on how their relationship has evolved, from childhood outings to meaningful conversations as adults.

articles.title: Enumerators in Delhi get morning duty on weekends to speed up Census 2027 work
articles.description: Delhi enumerators will work morning shifts on weekends to expedite Census 2027 amid heat and absenteeism concerns.

articles.title: Computerphobia, yes, it's a thing
articles.description: Our relationship with computers is complex; it's not ignorance that fuels our fear, but rather our profound dependence on technology. This intricate dance between attraction and anxiety often stems from our compulsive need for digital affirmation.

articles.title: Appeals court says Donald Trump's order banning asylum at border is illegal
articles.description: An appeals court on Friday blocked President Donald Trump's executive order suspending asylum access, a key pillar of the Republican president's plan to crack down on migration at the southern border of the US. A three-judge panel from the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit found that immigration laws give people the right to apply for asylum at the border, and the president can't circumvent that. The panel concluded that the Immigration and Nationality Act doesn't authorise the president to remove the plaintiffs under "procedures of his own making," allow him to suspend plaintiffs' right to apply for asylum or curtail procedures for adjudicating their anti-torture claims. "The power by proclamation to temporarily suspend the entry of specified foreign individuals into the United States does not contain implicit authority to override the INA's mandatory process to summarily remove foreign individuals," wrote Judge J. Michelle Childs, who was nominated to the ..

articles.title: Global campuses take root in India
articles.description: The landscape of education in India is changing as foreign universities set up shop locally. This strategic expansion is designed to enhance the quality of higher learning while making international diplomas more accessible.

articles.title: 'This isn't competition, it's theft’: Cohen urges DOJ to expose China’s ‘stealth’ strategy in Senate
articles.description: 'They stole U.S. data' claims Marsha Blackburn, pointing to alleged large-scale theft of U.S. intellectual property and sensitive data. Meanwhile, Mark Cohen argues that involving the Department of Justice could expose what he calls a hidden 'stealth strategy' fueling China’s growth.

articles.description: The poll was conducted between April 15 and 20 among 4,557 US adults, including 3,577 registered voters, and carries a margin of error of two percentage points.

articles.title: REPLAY: Press briefing by US Defense Secretary
articles.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Operation Epic Fury has achieved a decisive result in just weeks, focused on preventing Iran from developing nuclear weapons. He noted the mission is entering a new phase, giving Iran the chance to pursue a “good and wise” peace deal, while emphasising the strength of the U.S. naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, soon to be reinforced by a second aircraft carrier.

articles.title: Iranian foreign minister heads to Pakistan, signaling a possible revival of peace talks
articles.description: A top Iranian official is heading to Pakistan, raising the prospect of a new round of peace talks with the U.S.

articles.title: The blockade that couldn’t hold: Iran’s Phantom tankers buy time as Washington runs out of moves
articles.description: The blockade that was supposed to choke Iran is springing leaks everywhere. “The Blockade That Couldn’t Hold: Iran’s Phantom Tankers Buy Time as Washington Runs Out of Moves” tracks how Iranian oil is still slipping out to sea, even as U.S. warships ring the Gulf and Donald Trump vows to seal every route. Using dark fleets, switched‐off transponders, offshore transfers and shadowy flags of convenience, Tehran’s so‐called “phantom tankers” are buying the regime both time and hard cash.

articles.title: 'Giving up all my positions in AAP': Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak announces decision to join BJP
articles.description: In a significant political development, Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak announced his departure from the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) after a decade, citing disillusionment with the party's direction. He declared his intention to join the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

articles.title: '50 MLAs May Switch': Punjab Congress Chief Warns AAP Of More Defections After MPs Join BJP
articles.description: Warring said the move by Raghav Chadha and six other AAP MPs to align with the BJP reflected deeper structural weaknesses within the party.

Netanyahu says he was successfully treated for prostate cancer
articles.description: Netanyahu, 76, said that he had delayed the release of his annual medical report by two months to prevent Iran from spreading "false propaganda against Israel".

articles.title: How Raghav Chadha, Sandeep Pathak, Ashok Mittal Exit Triggers AAP Crisis
articles.description: In a major political setback for Arvind Kejriwal’s Aam Aadmi Party, seven Members of Parliament have resigned from the party and announced their decision to join the Bharatiya Janata Party. The move signals a significant internal rupture within AAP, raising serious questions over party unity and leadership. The mass exit, led by senior leaders, is being seen as one of the biggest political blows to the party in recent times, with implications for its strength in the Rajya Sabha and its broader national ambitions.

articles.title: 'Brutally beaten' by Kejriwal's aide, says Swati Maliwal after quitting party with 6 AAP Rajya Sabha MPs
articles.description: Beyond the allegation of assault, Swati Maliwal also claimed she faced sustained intimidation.

articles.title: Mamata slams Modi over Jadavpur University remarks, says they undermine premier institute
articles.description: West Bengal Election 2026: Mamata Banerjee hits back at PM Modi for calling Jadavpur University a symbol of anarchy, defending student protests as democracy.

articles.title: $400,000 Maduro bet: How a US special forces soldier landed in legal trouble
articles.description: The case has quickly drawn global attention, not just for the scale of the alleged misconduct but for what it reveals about the vulnerabilities emerging at the intersection of national security and decentralised finance.

articles.title: Kerala HC seeks govt reply on PIL alleging cruelty at cattle markets
articles.description: The petition has further alleged that cattle markets in the state are operating in violation of the advisory on 'Livestock in the Event of Heat Wave Situation' issued by the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying

articles.title: Glamourising The Gangster? Why 'Lawrence of Punjab' Sparked A Row And Has Now Been Banned
articles.description: The gangster's own family, politicians, police, and even the state government, slammed the OTT series for potentially glorifying criminality and fueling gang culture

articles.title: Amit Shah Targets Left Rule Over Kolkata ‘Slum City’ Remark, TMC Hits Back
articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has targeted the Left’s governance in West Bengal, alleging that its long rule contributed to Kolkata’s decline and its “slum city” image. The remarks, made during an election rally, have triggered a strong response from the Trinamool Congress.The TMC rejected the statement, calling it an insult to Kolkata and accusing the BJP of distorting the city’s identity for political purposes.Responding sharply, TMC MP Derek O’Brien said, “How dare you?” and criticised the remark as disrespectful.The incident has intensified the political confrontation between the BJP and TMC as campaigning gathers momentum in the state.

articles.title: AAP candidate injured in firing in Gujarat's Una, party blames BJP
articles.description: AAP has alleged that its candidate for local body polls in Gujarat's Una, Ajay, was injured in a firing. Party leaders Gopal Rai and Isudan Gadhvi have slammed the BJP, accusing them of a 'bloody game' and politics of fear to win polls.

articles.title: Man Who ‘Deliberately Infected Victims With HIV’ Gets Life Sentence
articles.description: Court hears man deliberately targeted vulnerable men and boys, leaving victims with lifelong trauma and illness

articles.title: Trump’s Secret Rant On ‘Keystone Kash’ EXPOSED After VIRAL Beer-Chugging Video | WATCH
articles.description: President Trump is reportedly unhappy with his FBI Chief Kash Patel after a viral locker room video showed him celebrating Team USA’s win by chugging beers. While Trump publicly defended Patel, a CNN report claims he privately criticised the incident and the backlash it triggered. With growing scrutiny and a $250M lawsuit already in play, the controversy continues to escalate.#trump #kashpatel #fbi #cnn #teamusa #usahockeyteam #theatlantic #donaldtrump #viralvideo #lockerroom #fbi chief

articles.title: ‘I feel like I’m starting over’: Woman opens up on life in India 6 months after leaving the US
articles.description: A woman who moved back to India after 17 years in the US shared how the shift felt both fulfilling and emotionally difficult.

articles.title: Live-in secret, marriage promise & pregnancy: Why Delhi High Court refuses pre-arrest bail in ‘false promise’ case
articles.description: A single-judge Bench of Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma declined relief to the applicant, observing that the prosecutrix appeared to have entered into the relationship “under a misconception of facts” arising from the promise of marriage extended to her.

articles.title: 'What mistake did I commit?' Pappu Yadav refuses to apologise for remarks on women
articles.description: Independent MP Pappu Yadav defends his statements on the Women's Reservation Bill. He asserts his commitment to protecting women entering politics. Yadav alleges exploitation of young girls by leaders and capitalists. He claims his remarks highlight the struggles women face in the political arena. The Bihar State Women's Commission has sought an explanation for his offensive statements.
